title: garlicky filipino gyoza fried-rice
description: a crowd-pleasing filipino dish featuring crispy gyoza and sweet garlic flavors. this recipe is a great weeknight meal option, requiring minimal cleanup and cooking time.

ingredients.name: gyoza wrappers
ingredients.notes: available in most grocery stores or online. make sure to store them in a dry place.

ingredients.name: soy sauce
ingredients.notes: use gluten-free soy sauce for a kosher-friendly option.

instructions: add 2 tbsp of vegetable oil to a large skillet or wok and heat over medium-high heat.
add the sliced garlic and saut	2 until fragrant, about 1-2 minutes.
add the chopped scallions and cook for an additional 1-2 minutes, until softened.
add the sliced apples and cook until they start to brown, about 3-4 minutes.
add the cooked gyoza and fry until crispy on the bottom.
use a spatula to break apart the gyoza into smaller pieces.
stir in the sugar, soy sauce, and peanut oil. season with salt and pepper to taste.
push the gyoza mixture to one side of the pan and add a small amount of oil to the empty side.
crack in a few eggs and scramble them until cooked through.
mix the eggs into the gyoza mixture and stir-fry everything together.
nigiri-shape the fried-rice into small balls, using a spoon to create the shape.
place the gyoza wrapper on a surface and add a small amount of the fried-rice mixture to the center.
shape the gyoza into small balls and place them on a plate or tray. repeat with the remaining ingredients.
serve the garlicky filipino gyoza fried-rice hot, garnished with nori seaweed sheets and sliced scallions.
use a steamer basket to cook the gyoza if you prefer a steamed option.
